knowledge.html:
Một số quy tắc chung

V (số ít) - Động từ chia số ít khi:

  • Chủ ngữ là danh từ va cum danh từ số it.
  • Chủ ngữ là các đại lượng chỉ thời gian, khoảng cách, tiền bạc, hay sự đo lường.
  • Chủ ngữ là các đại từ bất định: someone, anything, nothing, everyone, another...
  • Chủ ngữ la mệnh đề danh từ.
    Ex: All I want to do now is to sleep.
  • Chủ ngữ bắt đầu bằng "to infinitive" hoặc "V-ing".
  • Chủ ngữ bắt đầu bằng cụm từ "Many a".
  • Chủ ngữ là một phân số co tử số là 1. Ex: 1/2 is larger than 1/3.
  • Chủ ngữ là một số danh từ đặc biệt co hình thức số nhiều: môn học (Physics, Maths), môn thể thao (billiards, athletics...), tin tức (news), các loại bệnh (rabies, measles...), ten 1 so quốc gia va tổ chức (UN, United States, the Philippines...), loại đóng vat (ants, elephants...)
  • Chủ ngữ bắt đầu bằng Most of/All of/Plenty of + N (không đếm được/số ít).
  • Chủ ngữ bắt đầu bằng "The number of + N (số nhiều)".
  • Chủ ngữ bắt đầu bằng "None of + N (số nhiều)/No + N (số ít)".
  • Chủ ngữ bắt đầu bằng N1 (số ít) of N2.
  • Chủ ngữ la cum danh từ chi nhom động vật (số ít) mang nghia "bay, dan": flock of birds/sheep, school of fish, pride of lion, pack of dogs, herd of cattle.
  • A large amount/A great deal + N (không đếm được/số ít).
  • Neither (of)/Either of + N (số nhiều)
    Ex: Neither restaurants is expensive.
    Either of them works in this company.
  • Chủ ngữ là một tựa đề.
    Ex: "Chi pheo" is a famous work of Nam Cao.
  • Chủ ngữ bắt đầu bằng "A pair of + N (số nhiều)".
    Ex: A pair of pants is in the drawer.
  • Chủ ngữ là danh từ va cum danh từ số nhiều.
    Ex: Oranges are rich in vitamin C
  • Một số danh từ kết thúc bằng "s" những dung số nhiều: people, police, cattle, children, geese, mice...
    Ex: People are searching for something to eat.

Hai chủ ngữ noi nhau bằng "and"

  • Co quan hệ đẳng lập -> V số nhiều.
    Ex: Jane and Mary are my best friends.
  • Tuy nhiên, nếu 2 danh từ cung chỉ một người, một bộ phận hoặc 1 món ăn... thì động từ chia o số it. (Lưu ý: không có "the" o trước danh từ sau "and".)
    Ex: Bread and butter is their daily food.

Cấu trúc "both N1 and N2" -> V số nhiều

Ex: Both Betty and Joan are cooking for their dinner party.

V (số nhiều) - Động từ chia số nhiều khi:

  • Chủ ngữ là 1 đại từ: several, both, many, few, all, some + N (số nhiều).
    Ex: Several students are absent.
  • Chủ ngữ la "The + adj", chỉ một tập hợp người.
    Ex: The poor living here need help.
  • Chủ ngữ bắt đầu là một phân số co tử số tu 2 tro len.
    Ex: 2/5 are smaller than 1/2.
  • Các danh từ luôn đúng dang số nhiều: trouser, eyeglasses, jeans, tweezers, shorts, pliers, pants, tongs...
    Ex: The pants are in the drawer.
  • Chủ ngữ bắt đầu bằng Most of/All of/Plenty of/Some of/Majority of/The last of/One of/Part of/The rest of/Percentage of/A lot of/Lots of/A third of/Minority of + N (số nhiều).
    Ex: Most of people in the factory are male.
  • Chủ ngữ bắt đầu bằng "A number of + N (số nhiều)".
    Ex: A number of students going to class decrease.
  • Chủ ngữ bắt đầu bằng "No + N (số nhiều)".
    Ex: No people understand what he says.
  • Chủ ngữ bắt đầu bằng "N1 (số nhiều) of N2".
    Ex: The studies of how living things work are called philosophy.
  • Chủ ngữ la cum danh từ chi nhom động vật (số nhiều): flocks of birds/sheep; schools of fish; prides of lion; packs of dogs; herds of cattle...
    Ex: Flocks of birds are flying to its destination.

V chia theo chủ ngữ dau tien

Chủ ngữ được nối với nhau bởi các liên từ: "as long as, as well as, with, together with, along with, in addition to, accompanied by".

Ex: She, along with her classmates, is going to university this year.
Mrs. Smith together with her sons is going abroad.

V chia theo danh từ thứ 2

Either ...or...
Neither ... nor...
Not only ... but also...
... or...
... nor...
Not... but...

exercises.question: All the books on the shelf _______ to me.
exercises.explanation: 'All the books' is plural, so use plural verb 'belong'.

exercises.question: The trousers you bought for me _______ me.
exercises.explanation: 'Trousers' is always plural, so use 'don't fit'.

exercises.question: Mumps _______ usually caught by children.
exercises.explanation: 'Mumps' (a disease) looks plural but takes a singular verb.

exercises.question: The United States _______ between Canada and Mexico.
exercises.explanation: 'The United States' is a country name - takes singular verb 'lies'.

exercises.question: Physics _______ us understand the natural laws.
exercises.explanation: 'Physics' (a subject) takes a singular verb: 'helps'.

exercises.question: The police _______ the robber.
exercises.explanation: 'The police' is always plural, so use 'have arrested'.

exercises.question: Either you or he _______ wrong.
exercises.explanation: 'Either...or' - verb agrees with the nearest subject 'he' (singular) -> 'is'.

exercises.question: The doctor with the nurses _______ exhausted after the operation.
exercises.explanation: 'With the nurses' is an addition, not a conjunction. The main subject is 'the doctor' (singular).

exercises.question: Ninety percent of the work _______ been done.
exercises.explanation: 'The work' is uncountable, so 'ninety percent of the work' takes singular: 'has'.

exercises.question: Those who _______ to go with me, please raise your hand.
exercises.explanation: 'Those who' is plural, so use 'want'.

exercises.question: Salt and water _______ to wash the wound.
exercises.explanation: 'Salt and water' as a mixture/combination is treated as singular: 'is used'.

exercises.question: The news _______ bad last night.
exercises.explanation: 'The news' is always singular. 'Last night' requires past tense: 'was'.

exercises.question: Three-fifths of the police _______ in the school near the town.
exercises.explanation: 'The police' is plural, so use 'have been trained'.

exercises.question: _______ not only you but also he going to Japan?
exercises.explanation: 'Not only...but also' - verb agrees with the nearest subject 'he' (singular) -> 'Is'.

exercises.question: Five miles _______ not very far.
exercises.explanation: Distances are treated as singular: 'Five miles is not very far'.

exercises.question: Neither his parents nor his teacher _______ satisfied with his result.
exercises.explanation: 'Neither...nor' - verb agrees with the nearest subject 'his teacher' (singular) -> 'is'.

exercises.question: Writing a lot of letters _______ her tired.
exercises.explanation: A gerund phrase as subject is singular: 'Writing... makes'.

exercises.question: _______ everybody ready to start now?
exercises.explanation: 'Everybody' is an indefinite pronoun - takes singular verb 'Is'.

exercises.question: None of the butter in the fridge _______ good.
exercises.explanation: 'Butter' is uncountable, so 'none of the butter' takes singular: 'is'.

exercises.question: Miss White _______ her parents is going to pay a visit to the Great Wall.
exercises.explanation: 'Is going' is singular, so the linking word must keep the singular subject. 'As well as' means verb follows the first subject.

exercises.wrongSentence: One hundreds eight thousand miles is the speed of light.
exercises.correctSentence: One hundred eight thousand miles is the speed of light.
exercises.explanation: Numbers used as modifiers don't take plural 's': 'one hundred', not 'one hundreds'.

exercises.wrongSentence: The guest of honor, along with his wife and children, were sitting at the first table when we had a party yesterday.
exercises.correctSentence: The guest of honor, along with his wife and children, was sitting at the first table when we had a party yesterday.
exercises.explanation: 'Along with' doesn't change the subject. Main subject is 'the guest of honor' (singular) -> 'was'.

exercises.wrongSentence: The audience was enjoying every minute of the performance.
exercises.correctSentence: The audience were enjoying every minute of the performance.
exercises.explanation: When 'audience' refers to individual members acting separately, use plural verb.

exercises.wrongSentence: All the books on the top shelf belongs to me.
exercises.correctSentence: All the books on the top shelf belong to me.
exercises.explanation: 'All the books' is plural, so use 'belong' not 'belongs'.

exercises.wrongSentence: Five thousand pounds were stolen from the bank.
exercises.correctSentence: Five thousand pounds was stolen from the bank.
exercises.explanation: An amount of money is treated as singular: 'Five thousand pounds was stolen'.

exercises.wrongSentence: Neither his parents nor his teacher are satisfied with his result when he was at high school.
exercises.correctSentence: Neither his parents nor his teacher was satisfied with his result when he was at high school.
exercises.explanation: 'Neither...nor' - verb agrees with the nearest subject 'teacher' (singular), and past context requires 'was'.

exercises.wrongSentence: Working provide people with personal satisfaction as well as money.
exercises.correctSentence: Working provides people with personal satisfaction as well as money.
exercises.explanation: Gerund as subject is singular: 'Working provides'.

exercises.wrongSentence: Either the doctor or the nurses takes care of changing the patients' bandages.
exercises.correctSentence: Either the doctor or the nurses take care of changing the patients' bandages.
exercises.explanation: 'Either...or' - verb agrees with the nearest subject 'the nurses' (plural) -> 'take'.

exercises.wrongSentence: Every student who majors in English are ready to participate in the contest.
exercises.correctSentence: Every student who majors in English is ready to participate in the contest.
exercises.explanation: 'Every student' is singular -> 'is'.

exercises.wrongSentence: A number of students has failed the exam this semester.
exercises.correctSentence: A number of students have failed the exam this semester.
exercises.explanation: 'A number of + plural noun' takes a plural verb: 'have failed'. Don't confuse with 'the number of' which is singular.
